data.title: Microservices
data.description: Netflix utilizes a microservices architecture to break down its applications into small, independent services.
data.technical_spec: Services communicate over REST APIs or gRPC. Backed by Kubernetes for scalability.

data.title: AWS Cloud Infrastructure
data.description: Netflix runs its applications primarily on AWS, leveraging multiple AWS services for scalability and reliability.
data.technical_spec: Utilizes EC2, S3, DynamoDB, and RDS with multi-region failover capabilities.

data.title: Content Delivery Network (CDN)
data.description: CDNs are used to distribute content globally for low-latency streaming.
data.technical_spec: Leverages Open Connect, a custom-built CDN for efficient video streaming.

data.title: Chaos Engineering
data.description: Techniques used to ensure system reliability by simulating failures.
data.technical_spec: Tools like Chaos Monkey introduce controlled failures to test resilience.

data.title: Data Pipeline
data.description: Sophisticated data processing pipeline to power recommendations and analytics.
data.technical_spec: Incorporates Apache Kafka, Spark, and Hadoop for real-time data processing.
